name.description: A unique name for the recipe.

displayName.description: A display name for the recipe.

website.description: The website URL associated with the recipe.

isSetupRecipe.description: Whether this recipe is a setup recipe.

exportUtc.description: The UTC date/time when the recipe was exported.

requireNewScope.description: Whether the recipe requires a new scope.

ItemId.description: Optional unique identifier. When supplied and found, the existing connection is updated.

DisplayText.description: Human-readable name shown for the A2A connection in the admin UI.

Endpoint.description: Absolute HTTP or HTTPS endpoint of the remote A2A server.

CreatedUtc.description: Optional creation timestamp to preserve when importing exported records.

ModifiedUtc.description: Optional last-modified timestamp to preserve when importing exported records.

Author.description: Optional author name recorded with the connection.

Properties.description: Connection metadata grouped by metadata object name. Known built-in metadata currently lives under A2AConnectionMetadata, and extra objects are allowed for future extensions.

A2AConnectionMetadata.description: Known A2A client authentication settings stored for the connection. Additional properties are allowed for future A2A transport extensions.

AuthenticationType.description: How the A2A client authenticates to the remote endpoint.

ApiKeyHeaderName.description: Header name that carries the API key when AuthenticationType is ApiKey.

ApiKeyPrefix.description: Optional prefix placed before the API key value, such as 'Bearer' or a vendor-specific prefix.

ApiKey.description: API key secret. Recipe exports may sanitize this value, so imports can leave it empty to keep an existing stored secret.

BasicUsername.description: Username used when AuthenticationType is Basic.

BasicPassword.description: Password used when AuthenticationType is Basic. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

OAuth2TokenEndpoint.description: OAuth 2.0 token endpoint used by client-credentials, private-key JWT, or mutual-TLS authentication.

OAuth2ClientSecret.description: OAuth 2.0 client secret for client-credentials authentication. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

OAuth2KeyId.description: Optional key identifier used when AuthenticationType is OAuth2PrivateKeyJwt.

OAuth2PrivateKey.description: Private key material used for OAuth2PrivateKeyJwt. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

OAuth2ClientCertificate.description: Client certificate payload used for OAuth2Mtls. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

OAuth2ClientCertificatePassword.description: Password for the client certificate when AuthenticationType is OAuth2Mtls. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

AdditionalHeaders.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with each request when AuthenticationType is CustomHeaders.

Connections.description: The A2A connections to create or update.

Id.description: Unique admin menu content item identifier.

Enabled.description: Whether the admin menu is enabled.

ContentItemId.description: The unique identifier for the content item.

ContentItemVersionId.description: The version identifier for the content item.

DisplayText.description: The display text for the content item.

Latest.description: Whether this is the latest version.

Published.description: Whether this content item is published.

ModifiedUtc.description: The UTC date/time when the content item was last modified.

PublishedUtc.description: The UTC date/time when the content item was published.

CreatedUtc.description: The UTC date/time when the content item was created.

Owner.description: The owner of the content item.

Author.description: The author of the content item.

MenuItems.description: The list of menu item content items.

data.description: Admin menus to create or update.

Description.description: Administrative description of what the template customizes.

AdminTemplates.description: A dictionary keyed by template name. Each value has a Content property with Liquid markup.

ItemId.description: Optional unique identifier. When supplied and found, the existing data source is updated.

DisplayText.description: Human-readable name for the data source.

SourceIndexProfileName.description: Index profile that provides the raw source documents to ingest.

AIKnowledgeBaseIndexProfileName.description: Knowledge-base index profile that stores the normalized AI-ready documents.

KeyFieldName.description: Field name that uniquely identifies each source record.

TitleFieldName.description: Field name that contains the record title or summary label.

ContentFieldName.description: Field name that contains the main textual content used for AI retrieval.

CreatedUtc.description: Optional creation timestamp to preserve during import.

ModifiedUtc.description: Optional last-modified timestamp to preserve during import.

Author.description: Optional author name recorded with the data source.

Properties.description: Additional provider-specific data source metadata. No built-in metadata objects are currently exported here, but extra keys are allowed for future extensions.

DataSources.description: The AI data sources to create or update.

Name.description: Deployment name as specified by the vendor.

ModelName.description: Optional vendor model name. Defaults to Name when omitted.

ClientName.description: Client name for the registered AI provider.

ConnectionName.description: Connection name used to configure the provider.

Endpoint.description: Contained-connection endpoint alias for recipe imports. Supported by AzureSpeech deployments.

AuthenticationType.description: Contained-connection authentication type alias for recipe imports. Supported by AzureSpeech deployments.

ApiKey.description: Contained-connection API key alias for recipe imports. Supported by AzureSpeech deployments.

IdentityId.description: Contained-connection managed identity client ID alias for recipe imports. Supported by AzureSpeech deployments.

Endpoint.description: Contained-connection endpoint. Used by AzureSpeech deployments.

AuthenticationType.description: Azure authentication type. Supported values are Default, ManagedIdentity, or ApiKey.

ApiKey.description: Contained-connection API key. Required when AuthenticationType is ApiKey.

IdentityId.description: Optional client ID of a user-assigned managed identity. Used when AuthenticationType is ManagedIdentity.

Properties.description: Contained provider connection properties stored directly on the deployment.

anyOf.description: The deployment purpose. Defaults to Chat when not specified.

items.description: The deployment purpose. Defaults to Chat when not specified.

Deployments.description: The AI deployments to create or update.

ItemId.description: Optional unique identifier. If provided, used to find existing profile to update.

Source.description: The AI provider source name (e.g., OpenAI, Azure, Ollama). Required for new profiles.

Name.description: Unique technical name for the profile.

TitleType.description: How the session title is generated.

ChatDeploymentName.description: Chat deployment technical name for the AI model.

UtilityDeploymentName.description: Utility deployment technical name for the AI model.

CreatedUtc.description: Optional creation timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

Properties.description: Extended profile properties. Known metadata objects are listed here, and additional feature-specific objects are also allowed.

AgentMetadata.description: Known agent metadata for agent profiles.

Availability.description: Controls whether the agent is only available when selected or always injected into orchestration.

AIProfileMetadata.description: Known prompt and model tuning metadata for the profile.

InitialPrompt.description: Optional initial prompt automatically injected for chat profiles.

SystemMessage.description: The system instructions for the profile.

PastMessagesCount.description: Number of prior chat messages to include in context.

UseCaching.description: Whether distributed caching is enabled for the profile when the tenant allows it.

FunctionInvocationMetadata.description: Known tool selection metadata for the profile.

Names.description: Tool names that can be invoked by this profile.

AgentInvocationMetadata.description: Known on-demand agent selections for the profile.

Names.description: Agent profile names that can be invoked by this profile.

PromptTemplateMetadata.description: Known prompt template selections applied before or alongside the system message.

Parameters.description: Template parameter values. Keys are parameter names and values are strings.

Templates.description: Ordered prompt template selections for the profile.

AnalyticsMetadata.description: Known analytics and outcome tracking metadata for chat profiles.

Description.description: Goal description used for scoring guidance.

ConversionGoals.description: Configured conversion goals for chat analytics.

DataSourceMetadata.description: Known external data source selection for retrieval augmentation.

DataSourceId.description: Identifier of the selected AI data source.

AIDataSourceRagMetadata.description: Known retrieval augmentation parameters for an attached AI data source.

IsInScope.description: Whether answers must stay within the retrieved documents.

Filter.description: Optional OData filter applied to retrieved data.

AIProfileSessionDocumentsMetadata.description: Known session upload capabilities for the profile.

DocumentsMetadata.description: Known profile document attachments and retrieval behavior.

Documents.description: Documents explicitly attached to the profile.

DocumentTopN.description: How many top document matches to include.

MemoryMetadata.description: Known AI memory configuration for the profile.

EnableUserMemory.description: Whether user memory is enabled for the profile.

ClaudeSessionMetadata.description: Known Claude orchestrator overrides when the AI Claude Orchestrator feature is enabled.

ClaudeModel.description: Claude model override for this profile.

CopilotSessionMetadata.description: Known Copilot orchestrator overrides when the AI Copilot Orchestrator feature is enabled.

CopilotModel.description: Copilot model override for this profile.

IsAllowAll.description: Whether Copilot should run with the allow-all flag.

AIProfileMcpMetadata.description: Known MCP connection selections for this profile.

AIProfileA2AMetadata.description: Known A2A connection selections for this profile.

Settings.description: Profile settings. Known settings objects are listed here, and additional feature-specific settings are also allowed.

LockSystemMessage.description: Prevents recipe or editor updates from changing the system message.

IsRemovable.description: Controls whether the profile can be deleted from the admin UI.

InitialResponseHandlerName.description: Initial non-AI response handler to invoke before the main orchestrator.

IsOnAdminMenu.description: Whether the chat profile appears on the Orchard admin menu.

EnableDataExtraction.description: Enables extracting structured values from chat sessions.

ExtractionCheckInterval.description: How often data extraction runs during the session.

SessionInactivityTimeoutInMinutes.description: How long the session must be inactive before final extraction runs.

AllowMultipleValues.description: Whether multiple values can be captured.

IsUpdatable.description: Whether later session turns can update the extracted value.

Type.description: How the task result is generated.

AllowMultipleValues.description: Whether the task can return multiple values.

Options.description: Predefined options used when Type is PredefinedOptions.

PostSessionTasks.description: Tasks executed after the chat session closes.

ChatMode.description: Preferred input mode for this profile.

VoiceName.description: Optional speech synthesis voice for conversation mode.

EnableTextToSpeechPlayback.description: Whether assistant responses should be played back with text-to-speech.

Profiles.description: The AI profiles to create or update.

Name.description: Unique technical name for the template.

IsListable.description: Whether the template appears in the selection dropdown.

PastMessagesCount.description: Number of history messages to include.

ProfileDescription.description: Description of the profile's capabilities (used for Agent type).

Properties.description: Extended template properties. This includes the shared AI profile metadata objects plus template-specific metadata and template-stored settings objects, while still allowing additional feature-specific objects.

CopilotSessionMetadata.description: Known Copilot orchestrator overrides when the AI Copilot Orchestrator feature is enabled.

CopilotModel.description: Copilot model override for this profile.

ProfileTemplateMetadata.description: Known profile-template metadata stored when the template Source is Profile. These values seed the generated AIProfile before explicit recipe overrides are applied.

ProfileType.description: Profile type created from this template.

ChatDeploymentName.description: Chat deployment name used when the generated profile needs a chat-capable model.

UtilityDeploymentName.description: Utility deployment name used when the generated profile needs a utility or background model.

OrchestratorName.description: Orchestrator name applied to generated profiles.

InitialResponseHandlerName.description: Optional response handler that runs before the main orchestrator.

TitleType.description: How new session titles should be produced for generated profiles.

WelcomeMessage.description: Welcome message shown when the generated profile starts a new chat session.

PromptTemplate.description: Optional Liquid prompt template copied into the generated profile.

PromptSubject.description: Optional subject used with the prompt template.

SystemMessage.description: Base system message copied into generated profiles.

Temperature.description: Sampling temperature applied to generated profiles.

TopP.description: Nucleus sampling threshold applied to generated profiles.

FrequencyPenalty.description: Frequency penalty applied to generated profiles.

PresencePenalty.description: Presence penalty applied to generated profiles.

MaxOutputTokens.description: Maximum output tokens copied into generated profiles.

PastMessagesCount.description: Number of prior chat messages that generated profiles should include in context.

ToolNames.description: Tool names automatically selected for generated profiles.

AgentNames.description: On-demand agent profile names automatically selected for generated profiles.

Description.description: Profile description copied into the generated profile.

AgentAvailability.description: Agent availability applied when the generated profile type is Agent.

SystemPromptTemplateMetadata.description: Known system-prompt template metadata stored when the template Source is SystemPrompt.

SystemMessage.description: System message contributed by this reusable system-prompt template.

LockSystemMessage.description: Prevents recipe or editor updates from changing the system message.

Properties.description: Provider-specific connection metadata grouped by metadata object name.

Connections.description: The AI provider connections to create or update.

DisplayName.description: Display caption shown for the Azure AD login option.

TenantId.description: Azure AD tenant identifier that owns the application.

CallbackPath.description: Relative callback path that Azure AD redirects back to after sign-in.

Indices.description: Azure AI Search index definitions to create. Each object is forwarded to the Azure AI index recipe handler.

IncludeAll.description: When true, rebuild all Azure AI Search indexes and ignore the Indices list.

Indices.description: Specific Azure AI Search index names to rebuild when IncludeAll is false.

IncludeAll.description: When true, reset all Azure AI Search indexes and ignore the Indices list.

Indices.description: Specific Azure AI Search index names to reset when IncludeAll is false.

Commands.description: Commands to execute in order. Each entry is a single command line string.

ContentItemId.description: The unique identifier for the content item.

ContentItemVersionId.description: The version identifier for the content item.

DisplayText.description: The display text for the content item.

Latest.description: Whether this is the latest version.

Published.description: Whether this content item is published.

ModifiedUtc.description: The UTC date/time when the content item was last modified.

CreatedUtc.description: The UTC date/time when the content item was created.

Owner.description: The owner of the content item.

Author.description: The author of the content item.

data.description: Content items to import or update. Each item must match the schema for its content type.

ContentTypes.description: List of content types. Fields must be attached through a content part whose PartName matches the content type's Name. Those parts must also appear in the ContentPartFieldDefinitionRecords inside ContentParts. Fields cannot be used directly in PartName.

DisplayName.description: Display name shown in the Orchard admin UI.

Creatable.description: Whether editors can create new items of this type.

Listable.description: Whether items of this type appear in content lists.

Draftable.description: Whether items of this type support drafts.

ContentTypeSettings.description: Standard Orchard Core content type settings.

Settings.description: Content type settings object, including standard Orchard ContentTypeSettings.

PartName.description: Attached part type name. Known part names are enumerated when available.

Name.description: Attachment name used on the content type. Usually matches PartName.

DisplayName.description: Display label shown in the admin UI.

Position.description: Placement position used by Orchard when rendering the part or field.

DisplayMode.description: Display mode selected for the part or field.

Editor.description: Editor name used to edit the part or field.

Settings.description: Attachment settings for this part on the content type.

Pattern.description: Pattern for generating the alias. Must be valid Liquid syntax.

Options.description: Whether the alias is editable or auto-generated.

Pattern.description: The pattern used to build the Path. Must be valid Liquid syntax.
Pattern for building the preview path or display content.
This string must be a valid Liquid syntax

ContentTypePartDefinitionRecords.description: Parts attached to this content type.

Settings.description: Part definition settings, including part-specific settings envelopes.

FieldName.description: Field type name. Known Orchard field types are enumerated from the current tenant.

Name.description: Field name used inside the content part.

DisplayName.description: Display label shown in the admin UI.

Position.description: Placement position used by Orchard when rendering the part or field.

DisplayMode.description: Display mode selected for the part or field.

Editor.description: Editor name used to edit the part or field.

Settings.description: Field definition settings, including Orchard placement/editor settings and field-specific settings envelopes.

Pattern.description: This string must be valid Liquid syntax when provided.

ContentPartFieldDefinitionRecords.description: Field definitions that belong to this reusable part.

Profiles.description: The AI profiles to create or update from Profile templates. Each entry starts from the selected template and then applies any explicitly provided overrides.

Id.description: Optional unique identifier for the index profile.

ProviderName.description: Index provider name, such as Lucene, Elasticsearch, or AzureAI.

Type.description: Index profile type used by the target feature or indexing task.

Indexes.description: Index profiles to create or update.

then.description: Each additional property is a custom settings content type name with its content item data.

ContentType.description: Custom user settings content type to create or update for the user.

items.description: Single custom user settings content item payload.

user-custom-user-settings.description: Custom user settings content items assigned to the user.

items.description: Custom settings group for one user.

additionalProperties.description: Groups of users and their custom settings items.

then.description: Each additional property can use any collection name and must contain an array of user custom settings entries.

IncludeAll.description: When true, all deployments will be removed.

DeploymentNames.description: Specific deployment names to delete. Ignored when IncludeAll is true.

ContentParts.description: Reusable content part names to delete.

Step.description: Raw deployment step payload passed to the step serializer.

Steps.description: Deployment steps that belong to the plan.

Plans.description: Deployment plans to create or update.

Context.description: Optional localization context used to disambiguate the translation key.

Key.description: Translation key to replace or add.

Value.description: Localized value to store for the key.

Translations.description: Dynamic data translation entries to import.

IncludeAll.description: When true, rebuild all Elasticsearch indexes and ignore the Indices list.

Indices.description: Specific Elasticsearch index names to rebuild when IncludeAll is false.

IncludeAll.description: When true, reset all Elasticsearch indexes and ignore the Indices list.

Indices.description: Specific Elasticsearch index names to reset when IncludeAll is false.
Elasticsearch index definitions to create or update. Each object is forwarded to the index settings handler.

SdkJs.description: Optional URL override for the Facebook JavaScript SDK.

FBInit.description: Whether Orchard should emit the FB.init JavaScript bootstrap call.

FBInitParams.description: JSON object serialized as the arguments for FB.init.

Version.description: Facebook Graph API version to target, for example 'v20.0'.

CallbackPath.description: Relative callback path that Facebook redirects back to after sign-in.

Id.description: Optional unique identifier for the feature profile.

Name.description: Display name of the feature profile.

FeatureRules.description: Rules that determine which features belong to the profile.

FeatureProfiles.description: Dictionary of tenant feature profiles keyed by their technical name.

CallbackPath.description: Relative callback path that GitHub redirects back to after sign-in.

Description.description: Administrative description of when the layer should be used.

Rule.description: A JavaScript rule expression, e.g. isHomepage().

Name.description: Rule name shown in the layer editor.

ConditionId.description: Condition provider identifier used to evaluate the rule.

Indices.description: Lucene index definitions to create or update. Each object is forwarded to the Lucene index handler.

IncludeAll.description: When true, rebuild all Lucene indexes and ignore the Indices list.

Indices.description: Specific Lucene index names to rebuild when IncludeAll is false.

IncludeAll.description: When true, reset all Lucene indexes and ignore the Indices list.

Indices.description: Specific Lucene index names to reset when IncludeAll is false.

ItemId.description: Optional unique identifier. When supplied and found, the existing MCP connection is updated.

DisplayText.description: Human-readable name shown for the MCP connection in the admin UI.

Source.description: MCP transport type. Known built-in values are 'sse' for remote HTTP/SSE servers and 'stdio' for local process-based servers.

CreatedUtc.description: Optional creation timestamp to preserve during import.

ModifiedUtc.description: Optional last-modified timestamp to preserve during import.

Author.description: Optional author name recorded with the connection.

Properties.description: Transport-specific MCP client metadata grouped by metadata object name. Known built-in metadata objects are SseMcpConnectionMetadata and StdioMcpConnectionMetadata, and extra objects are allowed for future transport extensions.

SseMcpConnectionMetadata.description: Known HTTP/SSE MCP client settings for remote MCP servers. Additional properties are allowed for future MCP client features.

Endpoint.description: Absolute HTTP or HTTPS endpoint of the remote MCP server.

AuthenticationType.description: How the SSE MCP client authenticates to the remote server.

ApiKeyHeaderName.description: Header name that carries the API key when AuthenticationType is ApiKey.

ApiKeyPrefix.description: Optional prefix placed before the API key value, such as 'Bearer'.

ApiKey.description: API key secret. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

BasicUsername.description: Username used when AuthenticationType is Basic.

BasicPassword.description: Password used when AuthenticationType is Basic. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

OAuth2TokenEndpoint.description: OAuth 2.0 token endpoint used by client-credentials, private-key JWT, or mutual-TLS authentication.

OAuth2ClientSecret.description: OAuth 2.0 client secret for client-credentials authentication. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

OAuth2KeyId.description: Optional key identifier used when AuthenticationType is OAuth2PrivateKeyJwt.

OAuth2PrivateKey.description: Private key material used for OAuth2PrivateKeyJwt. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

OAuth2ClientCertificate.description: Client certificate payload used for OAuth2Mtls. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

OAuth2ClientCertificatePassword.description: Password for the client certificate when AuthenticationType is OAuth2Mtls. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

AdditionalHeaders.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with each request when AuthenticationType is CustomHeaders.

StdioMcpConnectionMetadata.description: Known STDIO MCP client settings for launching a local MCP process. Additional properties are allowed for future transport options.

Command.description: Executable or shell command used to start the local MCP server.

Arguments.description: Command-line arguments passed to the local MCP server process.

WorkingDirectory.description: Optional working directory used when starting the process.

EnvironmentVariables.description: Environment variables injected into the local MCP server process. Keys are variable names and values are strings.

Connections.description: The MCP connections to create or update.

ItemId.description: Optional unique identifier. When supplied and found, the existing MCP prompt is updated.

Name.description: Optional stored prompt name alias kept for export parity. Recipe imports primarily use Prompt.Name.

CreatedUtc.description: Optional creation timestamp to preserve during import.

ModifiedUtc.description: Optional last-modified timestamp to preserve during import.

Author.description: Optional author name recorded with the prompt.

Name.description: Unique MCP prompt name. This is the authoritative name used during import.

Title.description: Optional human-readable title displayed by MCP clients.

Description.description: Optional description that explains when the prompt should be used.

Name.description: Unique argument name used inside the MCP prompt template.

Title.description: Optional human-friendly argument title shown to clients.

Description.description: Optional guidance describing what value the argument expects.

Required.description: Whether the client must provide this argument.

Arguments.description: Declared prompt arguments that clients can supply when invoking the prompt.

Prompt.description: The MCP prompt definition to create or update.

Properties.description: Additional prompt metadata. No built-in MCP prompt metadata objects are currently exported here, but extra keys are allowed for future extensions.

Prompts.description: The MCP prompts to create or update.

ItemId.description: Optional unique identifier. When supplied and found, the existing MCP resource is updated.

DisplayText.description: Human-readable title of the MCP resource.

Source.description: Resource source type. Known built-in values include 'file', 'media', 'recipe', 'recipe-schema', 'recipe-step-schema', 'content-item', 'content-type', 'ftp', and 'sftp'.

CreatedUtc.description: Optional creation timestamp to preserve during import.

ModifiedUtc.description: Optional last-modified timestamp to preserve during import.

Author.description: Optional author name recorded with the resource.

Name.description: Logical resource name exposed to MCP clients.

Title.description: Optional display title. Imports usually derive this from DisplayText, so this field can be omitted.

Uri.description: Resource URI or relative path. Imports accept full URIs such as ftp://{itemId}/path and may also accept the path portion that will be expanded to the final MCP URI.

Description.description: Optional description shown to MCP clients.

MimeType.description: Optional MIME type advertised to MCP clients.

Resource.description: The MCP resource definition exposed to clients.

Properties.description: Source-specific resource metadata grouped by metadata object name. Known built-in metadata objects are FtpConnectionMetadata and SftpConnectionMetadata, and extra objects are allowed for future resource sources.

FtpConnectionMetadata.description: Known FTP connection settings for ftp:// MCP resources. Additional properties are allowed for provider-specific extensions.

Host.description: FTP or FTPS server host name.

Port.description: Optional FTP server port. Common defaults are 21 for FTP and 990 for implicit FTPS.

Username.description: Username used to authenticate to the FTP server.

Password.description: Password used to authenticate to the FTP server. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

EncryptionMode.description: FTP encryption mode. Use None for plain FTP, Explicit or Implicit for FTPS, or Auto to let the client decide.

DataConnectionType.description: FTP data channel mode used for directory listings and file transfers.

ValidateAnyCertificate.description: Whether to accept any SSL/TLS certificate, including self-signed certificates.

ReadTimeout.description: Read timeout in seconds for FTP operations.

RetryAttempts.description: How many times failed FTP operations should be retried.

SftpConnectionMetadata.description: Known SFTP connection settings for sftp:// MCP resources. Additional properties are allowed for provider-specific extensions.

Port.description: Optional SFTP server port. The usual default is 22.

Username.description: Username used to authenticate to the SFTP server.

Password.description: Password used for SFTP password authentication. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

PrivateKey.description: Private key payload used for SFTP key-based authentication. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

Passphrase.description: Optional passphrase for the private key. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

ProxyType.description: Optional proxy type used when the SFTP server is reached through a proxy.

ProxyHost.description: Proxy host name when ProxyType is not None.

ProxyPort.description: Proxy port when ProxyType is not None.

ProxyPassword.description: Optional proxy password. Recipe exports may sanitize this value.

KeepAliveInterval.description: Optional keep-alive interval in seconds to keep idle SFTP sessions active.

Resources.description: The MCP resources to create or update.

TargetPath.description: Media library path where the file will be stored.

Path.description: Alias for TargetPath. Path where the content will be written.

SourcePath.description: Relative path from the recipe file to the source media.

SourceUrl.description: Absolute URL to download the media from.

Hint.description: Short note shown to editors explaining when to use the media profile.

Quality.description: Encoder quality percentage used by formats that support lossy compression.

BackgroundColor.description: Background color used by pad-style resize modes.

MediaProfiles.description: A dictionary keyed by profile name. Each value is a media profile object.

CallbackPath.description: Relative callback path that Microsoft redirects back to after sign-in.

ContentTypes.description: Optional content type filter. Omit this property to process every content type that contains a Media Field configured with the Attached editor.

DisplayName.description: Human-readable application name shown to users and administrators.

RedirectUris.description: Space-separated or serialized list of allowed redirect URIs.

PostLogoutRedirectUris.description: Space-separated or serialized list of allowed post-logout redirect URIs.

ConsentType.description: How user consent is handled for the application.

ClientSecret.description: Client secret used by confidential applications.

Name.description: Role name granted to the application.

items.description: Roles granted to the client application.

Name.description: Scope name granted to the application.

items.description: Scopes granted to the client application.

AllowPasswordFlow.description: Whether the resource owner password flow is allowed for this client.

AllowClientCredentialsFlow.description: Whether the client credentials flow is allowed for this client.

AllowAuthorizationCodeFlow.description: Whether the authorization code flow is allowed for this client.

AllowRefreshTokenFlow.description: Whether the client can request refresh tokens.

AllowHybridFlow.description: Whether hybrid response types are allowed for this client.

AllowImplicitFlow.description: Whether implicit response types are allowed for this client.

AllowLogoutEndpoint.description: Whether the client can use the logout endpoint.

AllowIntrospectionEndpoint.description: Whether the client can call the introspection endpoint.

AllowRevocationEndpoint.description: Whether the client can call the revocation endpoint.

RequireProofKeyForCodeExchange.description: Whether PKCE is required for authorization code requests.

RequirePushedAuthorizationRequests.description: Whether the client must use pushed authorization requests.

DisplayName.description: Display caption shown for the OpenID client login option.

Authority.description: Authority or issuer base URL of the external OpenID provider.

CallbackPath.description: Relative callback path that the provider redirects back to after sign-in.

SignedOutRedirectUri.description: Absolute URI the provider should redirect to after sign-out completes.

SignedOutCallbackPath.description: Relative callback path that handles the post-logout response from the provider.

Scopes.description: Space-separated list of scopes requested from the provider.

ResponseType.description: OpenID Connect response type sent to the provider.

ResponseMode.description: How the provider should return the response payload.

StoreExternalTokens.description: Whether external access and refresh tokens should be stored for later use.

items.description: Additional protocol parameters appended to the authentication request.

Description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this scope grants.

DisplayName.description: Display caption shown for the scope.

ScopeName.description: Unique scope name exposed by the OpenID server.

Resources.description: Space-separated resource names associated with this scope.

AccessTokenFormat.description: Format used when issuing access tokens.

Authority.description: Public authority or issuer URL exposed by the OpenID server.

DisableAccessTokenEncryption.description: Whether encrypted access tokens should be disabled.

EncryptionCertificateStoreLocation.description: Windows certificate store location that contains the encryption certificate.

EncryptionCertificateStoreName.description: Windows certificate store name that contains the encryption certificate.

EncryptionCertificateThumbprint.description: Thumbprint of the certificate used for token encryption.

SigningCertificateStoreLocation.description: Windows certificate store location that contains the signing certificate.

SigningCertificateStoreName.description: Windows certificate store name that contains the signing certificate.

SigningCertificateThumbprint.description: Thumbprint of the certificate used for signing tokens.

EnableTokenEndpoint.description: Whether the token endpoint is enabled.

EnableAuthorizationEndpoint.description: Whether the authorization endpoint is enabled.

EnableLogoutEndpoint.description: Whether the logout endpoint is enabled.

EnableUserInfoEndpoint.description: Whether the user info endpoint is enabled.

EnableIntrospectionEndpoint.description: Whether the introspection endpoint is enabled.

EnablePushedAuthorizationEndpoint.description: Whether the pushed authorization endpoint is enabled.

EnableRevocationEndpoint.description: Whether the revocation endpoint is enabled.

AllowPasswordFlow.description: Whether the resource owner password flow is allowed.

AllowClientCredentialsFlow.description: Whether the client credentials flow is allowed.

AllowAuthorizationCodeFlow.description: Whether the authorization code flow is allowed.

AllowRefreshTokenFlow.description: Whether refresh tokens can be exchanged for new access tokens.

AllowHybridFlow.description: Whether hybrid response types are allowed.

AllowImplicitFlow.description: Whether implicit response types are allowed.

DisableRollingRefreshTokens.description: Whether refresh token rolling should be disabled.

RequireProofKeyForCodeExchange.description: Whether PKCE is required for authorization code flows.

RequirePushedAuthorizationRequests.description: Whether clients must use pushed authorization requests.

UseReferenceAccessTokens.description: Whether reference access tokens should be issued instead of self-contained tokens.

MetadataAddress.description: Absolute URL of the OpenID Connect metadata document.

Authority.description: Authority or issuer base URL used for token validation.

DisableTokenTypeValidation.description: Whether token type checks should be skipped during validation.

Tenant.description: Optional tenant or issuer hint used by the validation handler.

Placements.description: A dictionary keyed by shape type. Each value is an array of placement objects with 'place' and optional filters.

Source.description: Query source provider, such as Sql, Lucene, or Elasticsearch.

Schema.description: Optional schema or projection payload consumed by the source provider.

Template.description: Raw query text or template executed by the source provider.

ReturnContentItems.description: Whether the query returns full content items instead of primitive results.

IncludeAll.description: When true, rebuild all index profiles and ignore the IndexNames list.

IndexNames.description: Specific index profile names to rebuild when IncludeAll is false.

executionid.description: Unique execution identifier used to track the nested recipe execution.

name.description: Name of the non-startup recipe to execute.

Values.description: Referenced recipes to execute from this step.

ContentTypes.description: List of content types. Fields must be attached through a content part whose PartName matches the content type's Name. Those parts must also appear in the ContentPartFieldDefinitionRecords inside ContentParts. Fields cannot be used directly in PartName.

DisplayName.description: Display name shown in the Orchard admin UI.

Creatable.description: Whether editors can create new items of this type.

Listable.description: Whether items of this type appear in content lists.

Draftable.description: Whether items of this type support drafts.

ContentTypeSettings.description: Standard Orchard Core content type settings.

Settings.description: Content type settings object, including standard Orchard ContentTypeSettings.

PartName.description: Attached part type name. Known part names are enumerated when available.

Name.description: Attachment name used on the content type. Usually matches PartName.

DisplayName.description: Display label shown in the admin UI.

Position.description: Placement position used by Orchard when rendering the part or field.

DisplayMode.description: Display mode selected for the part or field.

Editor.description: Editor name used to edit the part or field.

Settings.description: Attachment settings for this part on the content type.

Pattern.description: Pattern for generating the alias. Must be valid Liquid syntax.

Options.description: Whether the alias is editable or auto-generated.

Pattern.description: The pattern used to build the Path. Must be valid Liquid syntax.
Pattern for building the preview path or display content.
This string must be a valid Liquid syntax

ContentTypePartDefinitionRecords.description: Parts attached to this content type.

Settings.description: Part definition settings, including part-specific settings envelopes.

FieldName.description: Field type name. Known Orchard field types are enumerated from the current tenant.

Name.description: Field name used inside the content part.

DisplayName.description: Display label shown in the admin UI.

Position.description: Placement position used by Orchard when rendering the part or field.

DisplayMode.description: Display mode selected for the part or field.

Editor.description: Editor name used to edit the part or field.

Settings.description: Field definition settings, including Orchard placement/editor settings and field-specific settings envelopes.

Pattern.description: This string must be valid Liquid syntax when provided.

ContentPartFieldDefinitionRecords.description: Field definitions that belong to this reusable part.

IncludeAll.description: When true, reset all index profiles and ignore the IndexNames list.

IndexNames.description: Specific index profile names to reset when IncludeAll is false.

PermissionBehavior.description: How permissions are merged: Add (default), Replace, or Remove.

BaseUrl.description: Optional absolute base URL for the site.

Calendar.description: Optional calendar system identifier used by the site.

MaxPagedCount.description: Maximum number of items Orchard may return from paged queries.

MaxPageSize.description: Maximum page size allowed by Orchard paging APIs.

PageSize.description: Default page size used by Orchard lists and queries.

ResourceDebugMode.description: Controls whether resource debugging stays with configuration, is forced on, or is forced off.

PageTitleFormat.description: Page title format string, typically including placeholders like '{{ Site.SiteName }}'.

SiteSalt.description: Application salt used by Orchard security and hashing features.

SuperUser.description: User name of the Orchard super user account.

UseCdn.description: Whether Orchard should serve static assets from a CDN base URL.

CdnBaseUrl.description: CDN base URL used when UseCdn is enabled.

AppendVersion.description: Whether Orchard should append file version hashes to resource URLs.

HomeRoute.description: Optional route object that overrides the site's home page target.

CacheMode.description: Controls Orchard cache behavior for the tenant.

AIChatAdminWidgetSettings.description: Configuration for the AI chat widget displayed in the admin dashboard.

ProfileId.description: The AI profile identifier used by the admin chat widget.

MaxSessions.description: The maximum number of concurrent chat sessions allowed.

PrimaryColor.description: The primary color for the chat widget UI.

AIDataSourceSettings.description: Default configuration for AI data source retrieval behavior.

DefaultStrictness.description: The default strictness level for data source queries (higher values are more strict).

DefaultTopNDocuments.description: The default number of top documents to retrieve from data sources.

AIMemorySettings.description: Configuration for AI memory storage and retrieval.

IndexProfileName.description: The name of the index profile used for memory storage.

TopN.description: The maximum number of memory entries to retrieve per query.

AdminSettings.description: Configuration for the admin dashboard appearance and behavior.

DisplayThemeToggler.description: Whether to display the light/dark theme toggler in the admin.

DisplayMenuFilter.description: Whether to display the menu filter input in the admin navigation.

DisplayNewMenu.description: Whether to display the 'New' menu in the admin navigation.

DisplayTitlesInTopbar.description: Whether to display page titles in the top bar.

AuditTrailSettings.description: Configuration for audit trail event recording.

Categories.description: The list of audit trail event category configurations.

IsEnabled.description: Whether this event is enabled for recording.

ClientIpAddressAllowed.description: Whether to record the client IP address in audit trail entries.

AuditTrailTrimmingSettings.description: Configuration for automatic trimming of old audit trail entries.

RetentionDays.description: The number of days to retain audit trail entries before trimming.

AuthenticatorAppLoginSettings.description: Configuration for authenticator app two-factor authentication.

UseEmailAsAuthenticatorDisplayName.description: Whether to use the user's email as the authenticator app display name.

TokenLength.description: The length of the verification token.

AzureADSettings.description: Configuration for Azure Active Directory authentication.

DisplayName.description: The display name for the Azure AD login provider.

AppId.description: The Application (client) ID from Azure AD app registration.

TenantId.description: The Directory (tenant) ID from Azure AD.

CallbackPath.description: The request path within the application's base path where the user-agent will be returned after sign-in.

SaveTokens.description: Whether to save the access and refresh tokens in the authentication properties.

AzureAISearchDefaultSettings.description: Configuration for Azure AI Search connection defaults.

UseCustomConfiguration.description: Whether to use custom configuration instead of the default.

AuthenticationType.description: The authentication type for Azure AI Search.

Endpoint.description: The Azure AI Search service endpoint URL.

ApiKey.description: The API key for authenticating with Azure AI Search.

IdentityClientId.description: The client ID of the managed identity to use.

AzureEmailSettings.description: Configuration for the Azure Communication Services email provider.

IsEnabled.description: Whether the Azure email provider is enabled.

ConnectionString.description: The Azure Communication Services connection string.

AzureSmsSettings.description: Configuration for the Azure Communication Services SMS provider.

IsEnabled.description: Whether the Azure SMS provider is enabled.

ConnectionString.description: The Azure Communication Services connection string.

PhoneNumber.description: The phone number to send SMS messages from.

CanadaDnclRegistrySettings.description: Configuration for the Canada LNNTE-DNCL Registry provider.

ProtectedApiKey.description: The encrypted API key used to authenticate with the Canada LNNTE-DNCL Registry.

AccountNumber.description: The Canada LNNTE-DNCL account number used for registry lookups.

BaseUrl.description: The base URL for the Canada LNNTE-DNCL Registry API.

ChangeEmailSettings.description: Configuration for the change email feature.

AllowChangeEmail.description: Whether to allow users to change their email address.

ChatInteractionChatModeSettings.description: Configuration for the default chat interaction mode.

ChatMode.description: The default chat input mode for interactions.

EnableTextToSpeechPlayback.description: Whether to enable text-to-speech playback of AI responses.

ClaudeSettings.description: Configuration for the Claude AI chat integration.

AuthenticationType.description: The authentication method used by the Claude integration.

BaseUrl.description: The base URL for the Claude API.

ProtectedApiKey.description: The encrypted API key for the Claude provider.

DefaultModel.description: The default Claude model to use for interactions.

ContentAuditTrailSettings.description: Configuration for content-specific audit trail tracking.

AllowedContentTypes.description: The content types to track in the audit trail.

ContentCulturePickerSettings.description: Configuration for the content culture picker behavior.

RedirectToHomepage.description: Whether to redirect to the homepage when switching cultures.

SetCookie.description: Whether to set a cookie to remember the selected culture.

ContentRequestCultureProviderSettings.description: Configuration for the content request culture provider.

SetCookie.description: Whether to set a cookie with the determined culture.

CopilotSettings.description: Configuration for the GitHub Copilot-style chat experience.

AuthenticationType.description: The authentication method used by the Copilot integration.

ClientId.description: The OAuth Client ID for GitHub OAuth authentication.

Scopes.description: The OAuth scopes requested during authentication.

ProviderType.description: The AI provider type (e.g., OpenAI, Azure).

BaseUrl.description: The base URL for the AI provider API.

ProtectedApiKey.description: The encrypted API key for the AI provider.

WireApi.description: The wire API protocol to use for communication.

DefaultModel.description: The default AI model to use for Copilot interactions.

AzureApiVersion.description: The Azure OpenAI API version when using Azure as the provider.

DefaultAIDeploymentSettings.description: Default AI deployment assignments for each capability type.

DefaultChatDeploymentName.description: The technical name of the default chat deployment.

DefaultUtilityDeploymentName.description: The technical name of the default utility deployment.

DefaultEmbeddingDeploymentName.description: The technical name of the default embedding deployment.

DefaultImageDeploymentName.description: The technical name of the default image generation deployment.

DefaultVisionDeploymentName.description: The technical name of the default vision deployment.

DefaultSpeechToTextDeploymentName.description: The technical name of the default speech-to-text deployment.

DefaultTextToSpeechDeploymentName.description: The technical name of the default text-to-speech deployment.

DefaultTextToSpeechVoiceId.description: The voice identifier to use for text-to-speech.

DefaultOrchestratorSettings.description: Default configuration for the AI chat orchestrator.

EnablePreemptiveRag.description: Whether to enable preemptive retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) in chat sessions.

DisplayNameSettings.description: Configuration for how user display names are generated and which name fields are required.

Template.description: A Liquid template for generating custom display names when Type is 'Other'.

DisplayName.description: Whether the display name field is hidden, optional, or required.

FirstName.description: Whether the first name field is hidden, optional, or required.

LastName.description: Whether the last name field is hidden, optional, or required.

MiddleName.description: Whether the middle name field is hidden, optional, or required.

DncRegistrySettings.description: Configuration for globally enforcing do-not-call registry checks during content imports.

EnforceGlobally.description: Whether do-not-call registry checks are globally enforced for content imports.

EnforcedRegistryKeys.description: The registry keys that are always checked during content imports.

Subject.description: The subject line for the verification email.

Body.description: The body template for the verification email. Use {{ Code }} for the verification code placeholder.

DefaultProviderName.description: The name of the default email provider.

ExportContentToDeploymentTargetSettings.description: Configuration for the export content to deployment target feature.

ExportContentToDeploymentTargetPlanId.description: The deployment plan ID used for exporting content.

UseExternalProviderIfOnlyOneDefined.description: Whether to automatically redirect to the external provider if only one is configured.

UseScriptToSyncProperties.description: Whether to use a script to synchronize user properties from external providers.

SyncPropertiesScript.description: The script used to synchronize user properties from external providers.

ExternalRegistrationSettings.description: Configuration for external authentication registration behavior.

DisableNewRegistrations.description: Whether to disable new user registrations via external providers.

NoPassword.description: Whether to skip requiring a password for externally authenticated users.

NoUsername.description: Whether to skip requiring a username for externally authenticated users.

NoEmail.description: Whether to skip requiring an email for externally authenticated users.

UseScriptToGenerateUsername.description: Whether to use a script to generate usernames for externally authenticated users.

GenerateUsernameScript.description: The script used to generate usernames for externally authenticated users.

CallbackPath.description: The request path within the application's base path where the user-agent will be returned after sign-in from Facebook.

SaveTokens.description: Whether to save the access and refresh tokens in the authentication properties.

FacebookSettings.description: Configuration for the Facebook social integration.

FBInit.description: Whether to initialize the Facebook JavaScript SDK on the frontend.

FBInitParams.default:
status: true,
xfbml: true,
autoLogAppEvents: true

SdkJs.description: The name of the SDK JavaScript file to load.

Version.description: The Facebook Graph API version to use (e.g., v3.2).

EnableAIUsageTracking.description: Whether to track AI usage metrics.

EnablePreemptiveMemoryRetrieval.description: Whether to enable preemptive memory retrieval for AI sessions.

OverrideMaximumIterationsPerRequest.description: Whether to override the default maximum iterations per request.

MaximumIterationsPerRequest.description: The maximum number of tool-call iterations allowed per AI request.

OverrideEnableDistributedCaching.description: Whether to override the distributed caching setting.

EnableDistributedCaching.description: Whether to enable distributed caching for AI responses.

OverrideEnableOpenTelemetry.description: Whether to override the OpenTelemetry setting.

EnableOpenTelemetry.description: Whether to enable OpenTelemetry tracing for AI operations.

ClientID.description: The Client ID from the GitHub OAuth application.

ClientSecret.description: The Client Secret from the GitHub OAuth application.

CallbackPath.description: The request path within the application's base path where the user-agent will be returned after sign-in from GitHub.

SaveTokens.description: Whether to save the access and refresh tokens in the authentication properties.

TrackingID.description: The Google Analytics tracking ID (e.g., G-XXXXXXXXXX).

ClientID.description: The Client ID from the Google Cloud Console.

ClientSecret.description: The Client Secret from the Google Cloud Console.

CallbackPath.description: The request path where the user-agent will be returned after authentication.

SaveTokens.description: Whether to save the access and refresh tokens.

ContainerID.description: The Google Tag Manager container ID (e.g., GTM-XXXXXXX).

HttpsSettings.description: Configuration for HTTPS and strict transport security.

EnableStrictTransportSecurity.description: Whether to enable HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) headers.

RequireHttps.description: Whether to require HTTPS for all requests.

RequireHttpsPermanent.description: Whether to use a permanent (301) redirect for HTTPS redirection.

SslPort.description: The port number for SSL connections.

InteractionDocumentSettings.description: Configuration for AI document retrieval during chat interactions.

IndexProfileName.description: The name of the index profile used for document retrieval.

TopN.description: The maximum number of document chunks to retrieve per query.

AllowDocumentUploads.description: Whether users can upload document files in chat interactions.

AllowImageUploads.description: Whether users can upload image files in chat interactions.

Zones.description: The list of available zones for widget placement.

LocalizationSettings.description: Configuration for site localization and culture settings.

DefaultCulture.description: The default culture for the site (e.g., en-US).

FallBackToParentCulture.description: Whether to fall back to the parent culture when a translation is not found.

UseSiteTheme.description: Whether to use the site theme for the login page.

DisableLocalLogin.description: Whether to disable local username/password login.

AllowRememberMe.description: Whether to allow users to be remembered across browser sessions during login.

AllowChangingUsername.description: Whether to allow users to change their username.

AllowChangingEmail.description: Whether to allow users to change their email address.

AllowChangingPhoneNumber.description: Whether to allow users to change their phone number.

MemoryMetadata.description: Configuration for user memory in chat interactions.

EnableUserMemory.description: Whether to enable per-user memory in chat interactions.

AppId.description: The Application (client) ID from the Microsoft app registration.

AppSecret.description: The application secret (client secret) from the Microsoft app registration.

CallbackPath.description: The request path within the application's base path where the user-agent will be returned after sign-in.

SaveTokens.description: Whether to save the access and refresh tokens in the authentication properties.

OpenIdClientSettings.description: Configuration for OpenID Connect client authentication.

DisplayName.description: The display name for the OpenID Connect login provider.

Authority.description: The URI of the OpenID Connect identity provider.

ClientId.description: The client identifier registered with the identity provider.

ClientSecret.description: The client secret registered with the identity provider.

CallbackPath.description: The request path within the application's base path where the user-agent will be returned.

SignedOutRedirectUri.description: The URI to redirect to after signing out.

SignedOutCallbackPath.description: The request path for the signed-out callback.

Scopes.description: The scopes to request from the identity provider.

ResponseType.description: The response type expected from the identity provider (e.g., 'code', 'id_token token').

ResponseMode.description: The response mode used when communicating with the identity provider.

StoreExternalTokens.description: Whether to store external tokens for later retrieval.

Parameters.description: Additional parameters to send to the identity provider.

OpenIdServerSettings.description: Configuration for the OpenID Connect server.

AccessTokenFormat.description: The format used to create access tokens.

Authority.description: The base URI of the OpenID Connect server.

DisableAccessTokenEncryption.description: Whether to disable access token encryption.

EncryptionCertificateStoreLocation.description: The X.509 certificate store location for the encryption certificate.

EncryptionCertificateStoreName.description: The X.509 certificate store name for the encryption certificate.

EncryptionCertificateThumbprint.description: The thumbprint of the X.509 encryption certificate.

SigningCertificateStoreLocation.description: The X.509 certificate store location for the signing certificate.

SigningCertificateStoreName.description: The X.509 certificate store name for the signing certificate.

SigningCertificateThumbprint.description: The thumbprint of the X.509 signing certificate.

AuthorizationEndpointPath.description: The relative path of the authorization endpoint.

LogoutEndpointPath.description: The relative path of the logout endpoint.

TokenEndpointPath.description: The relative path of the token endpoint.

UserinfoEndpointPath.description: The relative path of the userinfo endpoint.

IntrospectionEndpointPath.description: The relative path of the introspection endpoint.

RevocationEndpointPath.description: The relative path of the revocation endpoint.

AllowPasswordFlow.description: Whether to allow the resource owner password credentials grant.

AllowClientCredentialsFlow.description: Whether to allow the client credentials grant.

AllowAuthorizationCodeFlow.description: Whether to allow the authorization code grant.

AllowRefreshTokenFlow.description: Whether to allow refresh token grants.

AllowHybridFlow.description: Whether to allow the hybrid flow.

AllowImplicitFlow.description: Whether to allow the implicit flow.

DisableRollingRefreshTokens.description: Whether to disable rolling refresh tokens.

UseReferenceAccessTokens.description: Whether to use reference access tokens instead of self-contained tokens.

RequireProofKeyForCodeExchange.description: Whether to require PKCE for authorization code flow.

RequirePushedAuthorizationRequests.description: Whether to require pushed authorization requests (PAR).

OpenIdValidationSettings.description: Configuration for OpenID Connect token validation.

Audience.description: The audience (resource identifier) that this application accepts tokens for.

Authority.description: The URI of the OpenID Connect identity provider used to validate tokens.

DisableTokenTypeValidation.description: Whether to disable token type validation.

Tenant.description: The tenant name to use for local token validation.

MetadataAddress.description: The URI of the OpenID Connect discovery document.

ReCaptchaScriptUri.description: The URI for the reCAPTCHA JavaScript script.

ReCaptchaApiUri.description: The URI for the reCAPTCHA verification API.

UsersMustValidateEmail.description: Whether users must validate their email address before activation.

UsersAreModerated.description: Whether new user registrations require administrator approval.

UseSiteTheme.description: Whether to use the site theme for the registration page.

ResetPasswordSettings.description: Configuration for the password reset feature.

AllowResetPassword.description: Whether to allow users to reset their password.

UseSiteTheme.description: Whether to use the site theme for the reset password page.

ReverseProxySettings.description: Configuration for reverse proxy header forwarding.

KnownNetworks.description: The list of known network addresses.

KnownProxies.description: The list of known proxy IP addresses.

RobotsSettings.description: Configuration for the robots.txt file generation.

AllowAllAgents.description: Whether to allow all user agents.

DisallowAdmin.description: Whether to disallow crawling the admin area.

AdditionalRules.description: Additional custom rules appended to the robots.txt file.

RoleLoginSettings.description: Configuration for role-based two-factor authentication requirements.

RequireTwoFactorAuthenticationForSpecifi.description: Whether to require two-factor authentication for specific roles.

Roles.description: The roles that require two-factor authentication.

SearchSettings.description: Configuration for the site search feature.

DefaultIndexProfileName.description: The name of the default search index profile.

Placeholder.description: The placeholder text for the search input.

PageTitle.description: The title displayed on the search results page.

SitemapsRobotsSettings.description: Configuration for including sitemaps in the robots.txt file.

IncludeSitemaps.description: Whether to include sitemap URLs in the robots.txt file.

Body.description: The body template for the verification SMS message.

DefaultProviderName.description: The name of the default SMS provider.

SmtpSettings.description: Configuration for the SMTP email provider.

IsEnabled.description: Whether the SMTP email provider is enabled.

AutoSelectEncryption.description: Whether to automatically select the encryption method.

RequireCredentials.description: Whether the SMTP server requires authentication.

UseDefaultCredentials.description: Whether to use the default system credentials.

EncryptionMethod.description: The encryption method for the SMTP connection.

IgnoreInvalidSslCertificate.description: Whether to ignore invalid SSL certificates.

DeliveryMethod.description: The delivery method for sending emails.

PickupDirectoryLocation.description: The directory path for storing emails when using the pickup directory delivery method.

TaxonomyContentsAdminListSettings.description: Configuration for taxonomy filters in the content admin list.

TaxonomyContentItemIds.description: The content item IDs of taxonomies to use as filters.

TwilioSettings.description: Configuration for the Twilio SMS provider.

IsEnabled.description: Whether the Twilio SMS provider is enabled.

PhoneNumber.description: The Twilio phone number to send SMS messages from.

ConsumerKey.description: The API key (consumer key) from the Twitter developer application.

ConsumerSecret.description: The API secret key (consumer secret) from the Twitter developer application.

AccessToken.description: The access token for authenticating API requests.

AccessTokenSecret.description: The access token secret for authenticating API requests.

CallbackPath.description: The request path where the user-agent will be returned after authentication.

SaveTokens.description: Whether to save the access and refresh tokens.

RequireTwoFactorAuthentication.description: Whether to require two-factor authentication for all users.

AllowRememberClientTwoFactorAuthenticati.description: Whether to allow users to remember their two-factor authentication on a device.

NumberOfRecoveryCodesToGenerate.description: The number of recovery codes to generate.

UseSiteTheme.description: Whether to use the site theme for the two-factor authentication pages.

UsaFtcDncRegistrySettings.description: Configuration for the USA FTC Do Not Call Registry provider.

ProtectedApiKey.description: The encrypted API key used to authenticate with the USA FTC Do Not Call Registry.

OrganizationId.description: The USA FTC organization identifier used for registry lookups.

BaseUrl.description: The base URL for the USA FTC Do Not Call Registry API.

Required.description: Whether users are required to upload an avatar.

UseDefaultStyle.description: Whether to use the default avatar styling.

WorkflowTrimmingSettings.description: Configuration for automatic trimming of old workflow instances.

RetentionDays.description: The number of days to retain workflow instances before trimming.

Disabled.description: Whether automatic workflow trimming is disabled.

Statuses.description: The workflow statuses to include in trimming.

Hint.description: Short help text shown to editors.

Usage.description: Example usage snippet shown to editors.

Categories.description: Editor categories assigned to the shortcode template.

ShortcodeTemplates.description: A dictionary keyed by shortcode name.

data.description: Sitemap content items or sitemap definitions to create or update.

Templates.description: A dictionary keyed by template name. Each value has a Content property with Liquid markup.

site.description: The theme ID to use for the front-end site.

admin.description: The theme ID to use for the admin dashboard.

TimeZoneId.description: IANA time zone identifier to store for this map.

OwnerId.description: The user id of the user creating the entry. Leave it blank to use the current user's Id.

Author.description: The username of the user creating the entry. Leave it blank to use the current username.

Maps.description: The time zone maps to create or update.

context.description: Optional translation context used to disambiguate the key.

value.description: Localized value stored for the key.

AccessToken.description: Twitter/X API access token used for authenticated requests.

Rules.description: URL rewrite rule objects to create or update.

Id.description: Legacy numeric user ID when importing from older exports.

PasswordHash.description: Existing password hash to import. Use only when migrating users.

EmailConfirmed.description: Whether the email address is already confirmed.

IsEnabled.description: Whether the user account is enabled.

NormalizedEmail.description: Normalized email value used by the identity store.

NormalizedUserName.description: Normalized user name used by the identity store.

ResetToken.description: Optional password reset token to import.

PhoneNumberConfirmed.description: Whether the phone number is already confirmed.

IsLockoutEnabled.description: Whether the account can be locked out after failed sign-ins.

items.description: User record to create or update.

Name.description: Activity type name. Start activities must be events; non-start activities must be tasks.

IsStart.description: Whether this activity is a workflow start event.

X.description: Horizontal pixel position of the activity node in the designer.

Y.description: Vertical pixel position of the activity node in the designer.

Properties.description: Activity-specific property bag passed to the workflow activity.

Activities.description: Activities that belong to the workflow type.

Transitions.description: Transition objects that connect activity outcomes.

data.description: Workflow type definitions to create or update.
